nominal_registration.claim_scope: ideal zero-gap direct mating for S1 field characterization; no assembly-tolerance claim
nominal_registration.axis_mapping: RF +z -> oa +x; RF +x -> oa +y; RF +y -> oa +z

included: full RF quadrupole from local z=0 through the first-forward exit plane
RF entrance plate and trimmed exit front plate
oa accelerator repeller, grid1, graded rings, grid2 and grounded shield
5 mm grounded-boundary buffer downstream of oa grid2
excluded: RF standalone detector and enclosure downstream of the handoff plane
particle tracing in field-characterization runs; the separate deterministic N=100 runtime reuses this geometry
local_domain.rf_trim_rule: The S1 RF solid and vacuum geometry ends at the first-forward handoff plane; standalone acceptance hardware beyond that plane is forbidden.
local_domain.downstream_buffer_rule: Use the shortest tested local-domain buffer that reproduces the frozen closed-shield accelerator field and is stable to the next longer buffer; do not infer that the full TOF analyzer is required before this controlled test.
local_domain.legacy_external_vacuum_diagnostic_role: historical evidence only; forbidden for selecting or qualifying the shielded candidate
local_domain.rf_side_boundary: continuous grounded cylindrical RF housing with retained candidate inner radius 19.776 mm and grounded end plates
local_domain.interior_vacuum_rule: Mesh only vacuum enclosed by the RF housing, centered ion aperture and oa accelerator shield. No 350 mm flight-tube cross-section or exterior vacuum is part of the S1 connector sweep.

port_sweep.execution_order: closed local-domain control first, then descending opened width; stop only for a solver failure, not for a failed physical metric
port_sweep.sampling_rule: Always include y=0, every regular 0.25 mm offset and the exact +/- port half-width; the closed control is evaluated over the frozen 1.0 mm formal source width.
port_sweep.selection_scope: largest theory-admissible characterization width retained only for N=100 physical-port function; no performance or Formal selection

component_resolved_diagnostics_required: Ex Ey Ez injection-axis line integrals

numerical_qualification.source_authority: oa_tof/config/modes/formal.json comsol accelerator sizes and oa_tof/comsol/oatof_build_mesh.m release-volume refinement
numerical_qualification.closed_control_rule: Evaluate the identical local domain without an injection opening over the full 1.0 mm formal source width to measure numerical bias; crossing a legacy field alert does not itself fail the connector.
numerical_qualification.convergence_rule: Compare final particle observables at 0.5 and 0.25 mm. Numerical uncertainty must be no greater than 20% of each frozen performance budget and must not change its classification; use 0.125 mm only if that rule is unresolved.
numerical_qualification.final_boundary_policy: Use the real grounded connector or enclosure boundary for final qualification; arbitrary vacuum margins are diagnostics only.
diagnostic_controls: closed local domain with RF grounded hardware present
same closed local domain with RF hardware omitted and all other oa geometry, boundaries and mesh settings unchanged
numerical_qualification.diagnostic_control_policy: The oa-only control may isolate RF-hardware influence but may not qualify an opened physical connector or replace the full joint geometry.
numerical_qualification.claim_limit: A field-reference alert is not attributable to connector performance until the closed control, opened outer-domain sensitivity and particle-performance calibration are qualified.

field_characterization_evidence.decision: The 1.0 x 0.9 mm port is field-qualified only to enter deterministic N=100 physical-port runtime. Legacy uniformity alerts remain diagnostic; no S1 PASS, resolution or Formal claim is authorized.

particle_function_evidence.decision: The N=100 dynamic functional chain passes with at least one detector hit. This is a pulse-enabled function extension on S1 direct-mating geometry, not S1 stage PASS, S2/S3 qualification, resolution qualification or Formal promotion.
